_comment: InitechDOS INT 21h Flat 32-bit Calling Convention -- LOCKED spec-data
(CLAUDE.md Rule 8). NEW locked file; companion to spec/int21h_register.json
(the COARSE function set) -- this file pins the per-register ABI + the
per-function input/output/error contract for the functions implemented so
far (the CONSOLE-output subset; file-handle/SFT functions arrive with
Ratification: beads initech-1f9 (literal `int 0x21` via a 32-bit TRAP gate,
type 0x8F, DPL=0, selector CODE_SEL=0x08, at vector 0x21; PIC at 0x28/0x30
so 0x21 is free). ADR-0003 DEC-04 (INT 21h surface, AH selects function);
ADR-0003 DEC-13 (controlled vocabulary -- no unlisted function).
AH-dispatch: function selector in bits 15:8 of EAX (the AH byte).
All pointer arguments are FLAT 32-bit LINEAR addresses -- no segment:offset.
Every `ah` documented here MUST exist in spec/int21h_register.json
status: ratified -- ADR-0003 amendment DEC-04a (OEA-ADR-0003-A1), ARB sign-off 2026-06-08 (beads initech-1f9)

gate.note: PIC remapped to 0x28/0x30 (spec/int21h_register companion; pic.h), so vector 0x21 is free; ground-truth Sec 5.1 / Sec 8 Risk 3.

abi.return_value: EAX (AL for single-byte; AX for 16-bit legacy; EAX for 32-bit)
abi.error_flag: CF in saved EFLAGS (CF=1 -> error; AX=error_code)
abi.cf_mechanism: dispatcher writes bit 0 of int_frame_t.eflags before iretd

functions.notes: Alias for 4Ch AL=00h in the single-task model: serial INT21-EXIT rc=00 + console line, then cli;hlt. No process model yet (beads initech-509.5).
Output DL to the CON device (console + serial); DOS stdout handle 1.
Walk bytes at EDX until '$' (0x24); emit each via the PUTCHAR path. The '$' is NOT emitted (DOS convention).
Version 3.30 per ADR-0003 DEC-12 / spec/dos_banner.txt. EAX low word = 0x1E03.

functions.cf: set on error (AX=error code; 0x0006=invalid handle for any handle other than 1/2 in the pre-filesystem phase)
functions.notes: Pre-filesystem subset: accept handles 1 (stdout) and 2 (stderr) -> CON only. Real file handles arrive with beads initech-509.3 (JFT/SFT).

in.AL: minor function (00h = Get Device Information; this milestone)

out.DX: device-information word for the handle (AL=00h, on success)

functions.cf: set on error: AX=0x0006 (invalid handle) for a bad/closed/out-of-range EBX; AX=0x0001 (invalid function) for AL != 00h (minors 01/06/07/08 deferred -- beads initech-4nbn)
functions.notes: AL=00h Get Device Information (DOS 3.3 PRM INT 21h Fn 44h Subfunction 00h): resolve EBX through the JFT->SFT layer (sft_from_handle, the SAME resolution do_write/do_dup use) and return DX = the device-information word. A CHARACTER device (CON, SFT_KIND_DEVICE) -> DX=0x80D3 (the canonical real-DOS CON word: bit15 ISDEV + reserved bit7 + bit6 not-EOF + bit4 special + bit1 ISCIN + bit0 STDIN). A disk FILE (SFT_KIND_FILE) -> DX=0x0040 (bit15 clear; bits 0-5 = drive number 0 == A:, our single mounted volume; bit6 = 1 = file-not-written, the PRM default -- sft_entry_t has no per-handle dirty bit yet). Bad/closed/out-of-range handle -> CF=1, AX=0x0006. AL != 00h -> CF=1, AX=0x0001; the set-info (01h), input/output status (06h/07h) and changeable-media (08h) minors are deferred per operator decision 2026-06-15 (beads initech-4nbn). Purely an SFT/device-flag query -- touches NO FAT, no block layer, no ATA. Ref: DOS 3.3 PRM AH=44h; spec device-info bit names in os/milton/int21.h (INT21_DEVINFO_*); beads initech-ro6c.

functions.cf: never set (handler does not return to the caller)
functions.notes: Emit serial INT21-EXIT rc=NN + console line, then cli;hlt (terminate == stop; no process model yet). In future: restore parent context.

in.CX: attribute (UNUSED, as in 3Ch CREAT; attribute semantics are a separate bead)

functions.cf: set on error (AX=error code: 0x0050 file already exists; 0x0003 bad path component / overlength; 0x0002 NULL/empty path; 0x0005 no write backend; 0x0004 SFT/JFT slot exhaustion)
functions.notes: Identical to 3Ch CREAT (create a zero-length file, claim an SFT FILE slot + JFT slot in WRITE mode, return EAX=handle, CF clear) WITH one added precondition: the target must NOT already exist. If it does -> CF=1, AX=0x0050 (ERROR_FILE_EXISTS), commit nothing. The existence check reuses the backend pure-locate open() member (no SFT mutation, no whole-file read) and runs AFTER resolve_dir_path succeeds and BEFORE the SFT/JFT alloc, so a rejection touches nothing; shares the resolve_dir_path seam so '\SUB\FILE' subdir targets work. The AH=59h dispatch wrapper auto-notes CF+AX. Ref: DOS 3.3 PRM AH=5Bh; ADR-0003 Appendix A; beads initech-kji0.

in.EDX: flat ptr to the OLD ASCIIZ 8.3 path
in.EDI: flat ptr to the NEW ASCIIZ 8.3 path

functions.cf: set on error (AX=error code: 0x0002 NULL/empty EDX or EDI, or source not found; 0x0003 bad/overlong path component on either side; 0x0005 dest name already exists in the target dir, OR source is a directory/volume-label, OR no write backend; 0x0011 cross-directory pair, old_dir != new_dir). On success: CF=0, AX left untouched.
functions.notes: SAME-directory dir-entry rename (operator decision 2026-06-15, DOS-faithful; beads initech-gnrc). The FIRST INT 21h handler that reads a SECOND flat pointer: real DOS passes the new name in ES:DI; on this 32-bit protected-flat kernel the segment is collapsed and EDI carries the flat linear address directly -- the SAME flat-pointer convention EDX uses for the primary pointer (abi.primary_pointer; abi pointer args are flat 32-bit linear addresses). do_rename resolves BOTH paths through the resolve_dir_path seam ('\SUB\FILE' subdir targets work) and rejects a cross-DIRECTORY pair (old_dir != new_dir) with 0x0011 NOT_SAME_DEVICE: cross-dir MOVE is DEFERRED to beads initech-ycb3 (it needs dest-dir growth + an atomic remove/add + a '..' fixup). The backend rewrites ONLY the matched entry's 11-byte name field; start_cluster/file_size/attribute/mtime/mdate and the FAT are preserved verbatim (Rule 11 -- rename allocates/frees nothing). A directory/volume-label source is rejected 0x0005 (no '..' fixup path yet); a dest name already present is rejected 0x0005 (the load-bearing reject -- a rename never clobbers an entry). The AH=59h dispatch wrapper auto-notes CF+AX. Ref: DOS 3.3 PRM AH=56h; ADR-0003 Appendix A; beads initech-gnrc (cross-dir MOVE: initech-ycb3).

listed_but_unimplemented_behavior.applies_to: an AH that IS in spec/int21h_register.json but is not yet implemented in this subset (e.g. CON INPUT 01h/06h/0Ah which need the keyboard; file/SFT functions 3Dh-3Fh which need beads initech-509.3)
listed_but_unimplemented_behavior.action: return CF=1, AX=0x0001 (invalid function) for now
listed_but_unimplemented_behavior.rationale: Distinct, recognized diagnostic -- NOT 'unknown function'. The AH is sanctioned by the locked register; the implementation is deferred.

unlisted_ah_behavior.controlled_message: MSG-DOS-0002 (Bad command or file name) -- the closest controlled diagnostic; rendered to console if bound
unlisted_ah_behavior.rationale: CLAUDE.md Rule 2 (fail loud); ADR-0003 DEC-13 (controlled vocabulary). NEVER a silent no-op, NEVER a hang.

frame_struct.name: int_frame_t (reused from os/milton/idt.h)
frame_struct.note: The INT 21h trap stub builds the SAME int_frame_t layout as the exception path. For a trap gate the CPU pushes no error code; the stub pushes a dummy 0 err_code + a vector=0x21 sentinel to keep the layout uniform.
